blogs.title: Spreadfast Social Suite
blogs.body: The past few years Spredfast has hosted a 3 day long Social Suite at their offices featuring cocktails, food and a great opportunity to get off your feet and recoup some energy for the night. As a grand finale they host the Social Stage at Moody Theater and tend to book some big name acts. To access Social Stage you'll need to grab a Social Stage wristband in person at the Social Suite. Our crew missed 2016's Social Stage party featuring Chromeo because someone forgot to RSVP until it was too late. In 2015 they featured an incredible performance by the Flaming Lips. A VIP wristband was required to access to the balcony viewing/open bar area, but for a show like the Flaming Lips (alright for any show that's not run by a conductor waving a baton around like he just don't care) the place you want to be is four feet from the stage hoping to get a chance to bat Wayne Coyne around in his giant beach ball. This year's Social Suite has been announced for March 10th-12 and requires a confirmed invite. You can sign up on their web-site (www.spredfast.com/socialsuite/) We have yet to see any announcements re: Social Stage
blogs.author: Kevin

blogs.title: One Night In Austin
blogs.body:
The Rise and Fall of Perez Hilton's One Night In Austin
post_content: What began as one of the best underground/unofficial parties of SXSW several years ago has morphed into a well curated music showcase with little fanfare and only the barest hint of the personality it's architect once displayed.

My first experience with Perez Hilton's "One Night In Austin" was years ago at an abandoned Safeway on the wrong side of I-35. 
 Based on the recommendations of a friend we arrived three hours early to stand in maze of cattle gates that covered the otherwise empty parking lot.



What the first-time attendee of SXSW Music needs to understand is that the final day of SXSW is something different. In fact the final day of each phase of SXSW has something special to offer--a handful of can't miss events that attendees and locals a like will clamor to be a part of. Most will undoubtedly miss these, but none-the-less find themselves



The last day of Music, seems like the last day music will exist.� As though the next day every musical instrument in the world will be found mangled and broken in a ditch under a pile of discarded beer and whiskey bottles amidst a pile of soiled under garments.� At the end of a week full of intriguing new bands, surprise appearances of modern superstars, and the occasional appearance of a legend the last Saturday of SXSW stands out.� Across the city a handful of parties are competing to be the Grand Finale of SXSW, and a couple hundred thousand music lovers are competing to be at those finales.



In 2015 we witnessed what happens when a party itself enters middle age. It was all about the music and the music was good.� The party however was completely lost. It started in daylight.�It was held in a two level concrete box whose only decoration was a projector beaming graphics on the curtains behind the stage.� There were no commemorative photo booths with fantastically gaudy props...no colorful lounge areas, no wildly costumed bartenders tossing out free neon cups full of some brightly colored specialty cocktail.� No, just a concrete cube with two bars at the back selling over-priced Budwieser products to an overly sober crowd.


The music was good. Money was raised for charity. Tove Lo showed off her breasts (and they were beautiful), Elle King whined that her reception wasn't nearly loud enough, because for some reason that's her thing. And the sort of fun time that your parents would have watching the Grammies on the living room couch was had by all. But it wasn't a FINALE. It didn't go into the deep hours of morning. No one leaving even seemed drunk. But by the time it was over, it was too late for any of the hours long lines at the venues which had stepped up to be the new finales. Their opening acts had already begun, and their venues long since filled to capacity.

These days Perez Hilton's One Night in Austin has found good music and a good cause, but it's no longer a party and if you're looking for a grad finale SXSW, you'll have to look for something that starts just a little later in the evening.

Note: If you plan on going, buy a ticket. Proceeds go to VH1's Save the Music Foundation and ticket-holders get priority entry. SXSW badge holders will be admitted after ticket holders depending on availability.

Last year we closed out at the Russian house...featuring a wall of vodka soaked oddities that looked like it came out of Dr. Frankenstein's lab.
blogs.author: Kevin

blogs.title: SXSW
blogs.body: There are pages and pages of RSVP lists and links scattered across the internet.� This year we started out� using the lists over at: Balleratx.com�� Their list is arranged in a simple spreadsheet by date.� They include a simple yes/no note to let you know if there is free food or drink at the party, but you'll have to check out the rsvp link provided for details.� Many parties that offer freebies do so with restrictions. Some limit free drinks� to the first 100 people in line.� Some offer freebies only during their happy hour or for the fist hour or two of the event to help build a crowd.� A few will require the downloading of an app.� It's a handy list, but lacks some key details about freebies and admittedly has issues on mobile devices, which could be a problem if you are relying on it as your schedule keeper when you're out and about at Southby.� This list is a fine place to start but it does not have a separate section for recently added parties which is a critical flaw as new waves of parties are announced. http://Keeganslist.com� Keeganslist includes party RSVP links arranged by date.� It tends to do well early in the RSVP process, and seems to hit most of the bigger parties but as RSVP season picks up, the list is slow to update and misses a lot of the smaller stuff.� It does include a handy "Recently Added" section at the beginning, but the site itself has an irritating pop-up email subscription request that is strangely slow to load.� We subscribed but it continues to ask us every time we come back to the page to see if anything interesting has been added. http://atxconcert.wordpress.com/2017/01/18/sxsw-2017-rsvps/ ATXConcert is our current favorite.� They don't have any spreadsheets or notes on freebies.� They are just a list of RSVP links arranged by date, with the ever essential "Recently Added" section at the top. They update frequently and include parties that Keeganslist doesn't.
blogs.author: Kevin
